chapter_title.en: Chapter 32 – Marriage of the Zimmy woman (under the responsibility)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/65)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/65)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ الْبَرْقِيِّ

text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْيَهُودِيَّةِ وَ النَّصْرَانِيَّةِ أَ يَتَزَوَّجُهَا الرَّجُلُ عَلَى الْمُسْلِمَةِ قَالَ لَا وَ يَتَزَوَّجُ الْمُسْلِمَةَ عَلَى الْيَهُودِيَّةِ وَ النَّصْرَانِيَّةِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid Al Barqy, from Usman Bin Isa, from Sama’at Bin Mihran who said,

‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the Jewish woman and the Christian woman, can the man marry her upon (as additional wife) the Muslim woman?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No, but he can marry the Muslim woman upon (as additional wife) the Jewish woman and the Christian woman’.
en.sarwar: 5. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Khalid al-Barqiy from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Sama‘ah ibn Mehran who has said the following: “This is concerning my question before him (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, if one can marry a Jewish or a Christian woman as a second wife, the first being a Muslim woman. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, but a Muslim woman can become a second wife, the first being a Jewish or a Christian woman.’”
